claims.claim: LinkedIn's 2026 algorithm is keyword-driven (semantic search) not hashtag-driven; it removed hashtag following entirely (phased out through late 2024); 0-3 hashtags is the norm and hashtags no longer boost reach; it leans on the Interest Graph over the Relationship Graph (surfacing posts to topically-interested non-followers); 'saves' are a top engagement signal ahead of likes; dwell time is a major ranking signal; document/PDF carousels and native video far outperform plain text for reach. The first ~60 minutes ('golden hour') are an algorithmic test window: engagement and author replies in the first 15-60 minutes determine whether the post is amplified, so being present to reply fast (and not stacking a second post same-day) is a key reach lever.
claims.raw_snippet: In 2026 LinkedIn's algorithm is keyword/semantic-driven; hashtag following was removed; reach is driven by dwell time, saves, and early comments; the first 60 minutes are a test window where fast author replies boost distribution; document carousels (~6.6%) and native video (~5.6%) outperform text; the interest graph surfaces posts to non-followers.

claims.claim: External links in the post body cost roughly half the reach of a native post in 2026. Putting the link in the first comment reduces but no longer eliminates the penalty (LinkedIn now also dampens first-comment links), so it is mitigation, not a free pass; native content outperforms linking out.
claims.raw_snippet: External links still suppress reach (~50-60%); the first-comment workaround now carries its own reduced penalty in 2026 and is partially patched, native content performs best.

claims.claim: LinkedIn's 2026 authenticity policy ('Keeping conversations real') expects disclosure when AI materially generated or transformed a post and attribution of factual claims; it demotes formulaic AI 'slop' (organic reach for pages flagged as AI-generated has dropped toward ~2%), while human-AI hybrid content outperforms pure-AI content (reported ~156% better engagement).
claims.raw_snippet: LinkedIn best practices for content created with the help of AI: disclose AI assistance, attribute factual claims, keep content authentic; AI 'slop' is demoted and human-AI hybrid posts outperform pure AI.

claims.claim: Israeli LinkedIn timing: Sunday-Thursday work week, best windows 07:00-08:30 (morning commute) plus lunch and early evening; Friday-Saturday (Shabbat) low. Israel has ~3.1M registered LinkedIn members (DataReportal Digital 2026), roughly a third of the population (registered members, not monthly active users).
claims.raw_snippet: Israel LinkedIn audience ~3.1 million registered members; the Israeli work week is Sunday-Thursday with Shabbat (Fri eve-Sat) low activity.

claims.claim: Hebrew hashtags must be a single concatenated string with no spaces and no underscores (#סטארטאפישראלי not #סטארטאפ_ישראלי); LinkedIn hashtags break at the first space or non-alphanumeric separator.
claims.raw_snippet: LinkedIn hashtags are a single token; spaces or underscores break the hashtag, so multi-word Hebrew tags must be written without separators.

claims.claim: Official reference URLs in the skill.
claims.raw_snippet: Reference URLs: https://www.linkedin.com/help/linkedin https://business.linkedin.com https://www.geektime.co.il https://www.calcalist.co.il https://startupnationcentral.org
